title: Overview
summary: What Treadstone is, who it is for, and how to choose between the Console, CLI, REST API, and Python SDK.

title: Quickstart
summary: The fastest hosted path from account creation to a running sandbox and a shareable browser handoff URL.

title: Create a Sandbox
summary: Choose a template, set lifecycle controls, attach storage when needed, and capture the fields you will use next.

title: Browser Handoff
summary: Generate, inspect, and revoke browser handoff URLs without inventing tokens or guessing subdomain URLs.

title: API Keys & Auth
summary: Understand session login, API keys, control-plane access, data-plane access, and selected sandbox grants.

title: Usage & Limits
summary: Read plan limits, compute remaining, storage quota, allowed templates, concurrency limits, and billing-period status.

title: CLI Guide
summary: Use the hosted CLI for login, sandbox lifecycle, browser handoff, and API key management with machine-safe JSON output.

title: REST API Guide
summary: Call the hosted control plane directly with API keys to create sandboxes, issue browser handoff links, and inspect usage.

title: Python SDK Guide
summary: Use the generated Python SDK with AuthenticatedClient and typed request models for the hosted API.

title: API Reference
summary: The user-facing control-plane contract: auth, sandboxes, browser handoff, proxy, usage endpoints, pagination, and error shape.

title: CLI Reference
summary: Root options, command groups, auth precedence, JSON mode, config keys, and the exact hosted CLI surface.

title: Python SDK Reference
summary: Real import paths, generated endpoint modules, request and response models, and sync or async call shapes.

title: Error Reference
summary: The stable JSON error envelope, common public error codes, and the recovery step that matches each failure.
